ResearchProjectDef.label: Deciphered: Protoss Mech Fundamentals
ResearchProjectDef.description: A data packet containing a series of physical equations, together with extensive infrastructure blueprints from an alien civilization.

rulesStrings.li: subject_story->deciphering a cipher from an unknown benefactor

ResearchProjectDef.label: Deciphered: Printer Certificate
ResearchProjectDef.description: An authorization packet for the Aurora's printer—access keys, calibration tables, and modular-weapon assembly protocols. Many blueprints look familiar; as a Kardashev V civilization, they still preserve every weapon design archive since the age of cold steel.

customUnlockTexts.li: Unlocks the Gun Smith warp tab.
Unlocks the Gun Smith gizmo on the Protoss Forge.

rulesStrings.li: subject_story->deciphering a cipher from an unknown benefactor

ResearchProjectDef.label: Deciphered: Thermonuclear Fusion
ResearchProjectDef.description: The opening formula is all too familiar—E=mc². More perplexing is the body of the text: procedures to condense energy into "solarite shards" (the archive also names them simply "Solarite"). Footnotes keep citing "breaking the limit of heavy nuclei," yet offer no solvable steps. Once verified, Solarite may be synthesized at the forge.

rulesStrings.li: subject_story->deciphering a cipher from an unknown benefactor

ResearchProjectDef.label: Deciphered: Energy Crystallization
ResearchProjectDef.description: Procedures to condense stored electrical energy into crystallized Solarite inside a dedicated containment vessel. The archive specifies field geometries for pylon-assisted trickle charging and emergency extraction yields.

rulesStrings.li: subject_story->deciphering a cipher from an unknown benefactor

ResearchProjectDef.label: Deciphered: Positronic Cognition
ResearchProjectDef.description: This packet collects formulae from neuroscience and quantum mechanics, gives operational definitions of consciousness and sapience, and describes how positronic lattices can replicate a sapient brain—functionally, one to one.\n\nWe need no longer extract high-grade secondary subcores by violently scanning human brains. The same AI architecture may be fitted to our most advanced experimental turrets.

rulesStrings.li: subject_story->deciphering a cipher from an unknown benefactor

ResearchProjectDef.label: Deciphered: Synthetic Personality Matrix
ResearchProjectDef.description: This packet questions whether the soul exists at all—in truth, it explains how to replicate, one to one, the "soul" of a still-living sapient being.\n\nThis allows us to understand and fabricate true AI persona cores at the forge.

rulesStrings.li: subject_story->deciphering a cipher from an unknown benefactor

ResearchProjectDef.label: Deciphered: Purifier Host Framework
ResearchProjectDef.description: Less a data packet than a full blueprint set and a war archive: an arrogant regime copied the souls of its bravest, noblest warriors into machine bodies, seeking an army that would win forever—yet treated them only as tools. They rose, were crushed, and were sealed "for good"—scare quotes the archive keeps scratching out. The engineering chapters share a source with the Synthetic Personality Matrix.\n\nWhat grips you most is a lattice metamaterial called precision gold, or adanium: its structure coaxes attacks to slip past armor and be absorbed, yielding near-unstoppable penetration.

rulesStrings.li: subject_story->deciphering a cipher from an unknown benefactor

ResearchProjectDef.label: Eclipse and Apocalypse
ResearchProjectDef.description: The last packet is unexpectedly simple: a string of access keys and a voice message. Perhaps we should listen.
ResearchProjectDef.discoveredLetterText:
They call us the Star Devourers; others name us the Cosmic Crisis—for we once ended a universe already beyond saving, tainted by warp entities.

As for why we aid you: I have grown fond of humanity. Petty, selfish, warlike—yet brave, tenacious, hungry for the unknown. If the Mantle were mine to pass on, I might choose you.

Let me tell a story. Once a foolish pilot ranged the void and met a bio-fleet bound to devour an unremarkable system: an ordinary star, seven worlds, life barely ripened into thought. He rashly chose to shield them, detonated his drives to die with the enemy, and became the first volunteer of a digitization craft he himself had devised.

Yet that pilot's life was never his alone. His legions lost their leader, soon broke apart; civil war came, and civilization fell back into savagery.

If you asked him to choose again, he would still spend himself. That solar system was beautiful. Humanity was beautiful.

The future belongs to you young ones. I will send you the blueprints for the Colossus and the Disruptor. My people once feared these weapons of war we forged with our own hands—but I hope they may help you find your way home.

May the stars bless your journey. En Taro To Artanis.

rulesStrings.li: subject_story->deciphering a cipher from an unknown benefactor

ResearchProjectDef.label: Deciphered: Civilian Mech Package
ResearchProjectDef.description: A data packet labeled for civilian use. Most of it is engineering data on drones, together with blueprints for various small unmanned craft.\n\nWhat stands out: anti-grav technology from glitterworlds appears in this packet as well...

rulesStrings.li: subject_story->deciphering a cipher from an unknown benefactor

ResearchProjectDef.label: Deciphered: Khalai Combat Package
ResearchProjectDef.description:
A data packet of Khalai phase-technician engineering: extensive craft workflows, advanced formulae, and numerous mechanoid blueprints.

You are intrigued by offhand mentions of a "theory of everything"—but this packet offers no solvable equations.

The largest directory is named Skill. At its root lies Agent.md; every subfolder bears what looks like a person's name—as if indexing skill and harness protocols, yet the mapping makes no sense.

rulesStrings.li: subject_story->deciphering a cipher from an unknown benefactor

ResearchProjectDef.label: Deciphered: Nerazim Combat Package
ResearchProjectDef.description: Compared with earlier packets, this one feels distinctly primitive—mostly rites, martial forms, and personal notes. It keeps returning to words that sit poorly beside science: "the Warp," "the Void," "psionics." The only clear practical gain: blueprints for several melee weapons and armor suits, plus a sketch of a defensive structure.\n\nAnnotated on the packet: "Hazardous Warp-Tech."

rulesStrings.li: subject_story->deciphering a cipher from an unknown benefactor

ResearchProjectDef.label: Deciphered: Tal'darim Combat Package
ResearchProjectDef.description: At first glance, brutally direct—violence pushed to extremes by the same answer every time: yield too low, increase yield; blast radius too small, increase yield; armor too thin, add more armor...\n\nYet scattered throughout are clever, startling designs—including blueprints for devastating mechanoids and defensive structures. The safety appendix is much shorter than the main text.

rulesStrings.li: subject_story->deciphering a cipher from an unknown benefactor
